entities.name: danuglipron
entities.context: Danuglipron (PF-06882961) was developed. Danuglipron, derived from this chemical series, reached Phase 2/3 clinical trials for type 2 diabetes and obesity. Pfizer subsequently discontinued the twice-daily formulation but continues development of a once-daily formulation.

entities.name: orforglipron
entities.context: A separate oral small-molecule GLP-1 agonist, orforglipron (LY3502970, originally OWL-833), is being developed by Eli Lilly with the molecular formula C48H48F2N10O5 and molecular weight 882.96, and has demonstrated significant weight loss and HbA1c reduction in Phase 3 trials (ACHIEVE program). Orforglipron has CAS Registry Number 2212020-52-3.

entities.name: semaglutide
entities.context: Unlike peptide-based GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide, liraglutide, tirzepatide), these are orally bioavailable nonpeptide small molecules that activate the GLP-1 receptor through an allosteric mechanism.

entities.name: liraglutide
entities.context: Unlike peptide-based GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide, liraglutide, tirzepatide), these are orally bioavailable nonpeptide small molecules that activate the GLP-1 receptor through an allosteric mechanism.

entities.name: tirzepatide

entities.name: benzimidazole-based GLP-1 receptor agonist scaffold
entities.context: The invention describes 6-carboxylic acids of benzimidazoles and 4-aza-, 5-aza-, 7-aza-, and 4,7-diaza-benzimidazoles as small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonists, processes for their synthesis, and methods of administering said compounds to mammals in need thereof.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor
entities.context: These are orally bioavailable nonpeptide small molecules that activate the GLP-1 receptor through an allosteric mechanism. The compounds bind within a transmembrane pocket distinct from the orthosteric peptide-binding domain, stabilizing an active receptor conformation that couples to Gs proteins and drives intracellular cAMP accumulation.

entities.context: Stabilizing an active receptor conformation that couples to Gs proteins and drives intracellular cAMP accumulation.

entities.context: Making them resistant to proteolytic degradation by enzymes such as dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) and neutral endopeptidases.

relations.evidence: The invention describes 6-carboxylic acids of benzimidazoles and 4-aza-, 5-aza-, 7-aza-, and 4,7-diaza-benzimidazoles as small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonists.

These are orally bioavailable nonpeptide small molecules that activate the GLP-1 receptor through an allosteric mechanism. The compounds bind within a transmembrane pocket distinct from the orthosteric peptide-binding domain, stabilizing an active receptor conformation that couples to Gs proteins and drives intracellular cAMP accumulation.

This patent covers the chemical class from which danuglipron (PF-06882961) was developed. These are orally bioavailable nonpeptide small molecules that activate the GLP-1 receptor through an allosteric mechanism.

A separate oral small-molecule GLP-1 agonist, orforglipron (LY3502970, originally OWL-833), is being developed by Eli Lilly.

The development of oral nonpeptide GLP-1 agonists represents a significant advance in the GLP-1 receptor agonist class, potentially expanding patient access by eliminating the need for injection and improving treatment adherence.
Orforglipron has demonstrated significant weight loss and HbA1c reduction in Phase 3 trials (ACHIEVE program).

A separate oral small-molecule GLP-1 agonist, orforglipron (LY3502970, originally OWL-833), is being developed by Eli Lilly.
Orforglipron has demonstrated significant weight loss and HbA1c reduction in Phase 3 trials (ACHIEVE program).

The compounds bind within a transmembrane pocket distinct from the orthosteric peptide-binding domain, stabilizing an active receptor conformation that couples to Gs proteins and drives intracellular cAMP accumulation.
Stabilizing an active receptor conformation that couples to Gs proteins and drives intracellular cAMP accumulation.
These are orally bioavailable nonpeptide small molecules that activate the GLP-1 receptor through an allosteric mechanism.

The compounds are characterized by molecular weights in the range suitable for oral absorption and lack peptide bonds, making them resistant to proteolytic degradation by enzymes such as dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) and neutral endopeptidases.
